title: Capabilities
description: WOP capability declaration returned from `GET /.well-known/wop`. Superset of the in-package `Capabilities` shape (PR 3b.1.1 in the reference impl) — fields beyond the in-package set are tagged `(future)` in `capabilities.md`. Servers MAY omit (future) fields without losing compliance.

protocolVersion.description: WOP protocol version the server speaks (e.g., `1.0.0`). Independent of `engineVersion` (which gates persisted-doc compatibility per version-negotiation.md).

schemaVersions.description: Active schema version per envelope type (e.g., `{ "prd.create": 2 }`).

clarificationRounds.description: Maximum total clarification envelopes per task before the engine forces the LLM to proceed.

schemaRounds.description: Maximum total schema-validation rounds per envelope before the node fails.

envelopesPerTurn.description: Maximum total envelopes the LLM may emit in a single chat turn.

maxNodeExecutions.description: Engine-side ceiling on total node executions per run. Acts as the upper bound for `RunOptions.configurable.recursionLimit`. Optional in v1; mandatory once CC-1's hard invariant lands. Sentinel `0` means "not enforced".

limits.description: Hard limits enforced by the engine. See capabilities.md §3 + WOP plan CC-1/CC-4.

extensions.description: Optional per-canvas-type or per-workflow extensions (additional envelope types, override limits).

implementation.description: (future) Identifies the WOP-compliant server (name + version + vendor).

configurable.description: (future) Per-run overlay schema — what `RunOptions.configurable` keys this server honors.

observability.description: (future) Hints about emitted spans/metrics/logs. See observability.md.

minClientVersion.description: (future) Minimum client SDK version the server interops with.

secrets.description: (future) G22 — host advertises secret/credential resolution. Clients gate BYOK flows on this. Hosts that don't store credentials return `supported: false`.

supported.description: Host has any secret-resolution at all.

scopes.description: Subset of scopes the host implements. Tenant-scoped secrets are workspace-shared; user-scoped are per-end-user; run-scoped are ephemeral per-run.

resolution.description: Resolution mode. v1.x supports only `host-managed` (clients reference stored secrets via opaque ids); reserved for future modes.

runtimeCapabilities.description: (future) G23 — host-advertised opaque capability ids that NodeModules may declare in `NodeModule.requires`. Naming convention: dotted, domain-scoped (`chat.sendPrompt`, `canvas.write`, `secrets.byok`). Provider value shapes are documented per-capability alongside consumers, NOT in the protocol package — the protocol owns the *check*, not domain provider contracts. A client that submits a workflow whose nodes declare a `requires` entry SHOULD first verify the host advertises that capability; a host that lacks a capability MUST refuse to dispatch nodes that declare it, terminating the run with `RunSnapshot.error.code = 'capability_not_provided'`. See `capabilities.md` §"Runtime capabilities (future)".

aiProviders.description: (future) G22 — companion to `secrets`. Advertises which AI providers the host's AI-proxy can route to and which permit BYOK.

supported.description: Provider ids the host's AI-proxy can route to. Conventional ids: `anthropic`, `openai`, `gemini`, `mistral`, `cohere`, `vertex`, `bedrock`. Hosts MAY add vendor-prefixed extensions.

byok.description: Subset of `supported` for which BYOK is permitted. Empty array → all calls use platform-managed keys; non-empty → clients MAY pass `ai.credentialRef` in `RunOptions.configurable` for matching providers.

policies.description: (future) G22 — host-side policy enforcement modes for per-provider gating. Omitted → no enforcement; clients see only `optional` semantics. When present, MUST declare `modes` — an empty `{}` is not a valid third state. See `capabilities.md` §`aiProviders.policies`.

modes.description: Subset of policy modes this host can enforce. `disabled` = provider may not be used; `optional` = no restriction (default); `required` = BYOK required; `restricted` = model must match the policy's `allowedModels` glob list. Hosts MAY support a subset; clients MUST tolerate any subset.

scopes.description: Resolution layers the host evaluates. Conventional ids: `workspace`, `project`, `canvas-type`. Precedence is host-defined and SHOULD be documented per-deployment.

errorCode.description: Wire-format error code returned on denial. Defaults to `provider_policy_denied`. Reserved for hosts that need a vendor-prefixed alias.

testing.description: Testing-mode capabilities advertised to clients (closes F1). Lets conformance suites + dev clients discover which mock providers the server supports and which API-key prefix denotes test keys (since mock providers are test-keys-only).

mockProviders.description: Mock-provider IDs this server recognizes via `RunOptions.configurable.mockProvider.id`. Servers claiming conformance v0.X+ MUST include `stream-text`. Other canonical providers (`tool-calls`, `error`, `usage-only`) are recommended. Implementations MAY add their own (vendor-prefixed).

testKeyPrefix.description: API-key prefix that marks a key as test-mode. Reference impl uses `hk_test_`. Mock providers + other test-only surfaces gate on this prefix.

forceEngineVersionRange.description: Range of engine versions the server can be forced into via the `X-Force-Engine-Version` request header (test-keys-only). Used by the conformance suite to verify forward-compat fold-best-effort across the version-negotiation matrix. Closes F5.

min.description: Lowest forceable engine version. Typically (current - 1) so back-compat is exercised.

max.description: Highest forceable engine version. Typically (current + 1) so forward-compat is exercised.

compliance.description: Privacy / compliance behavior advertised to clients (closes O5). Lets consumers know what masking mode is in effect so a `"[REDACTED]"` value in event log payloads is recognizable as a server-side mask vs an upstream null.

defaultMode.description: Server's default masking mode for fields marked sensitive. `mask` (default): replace with `"[REDACTED]"`. `omit`: drop the field entirely. `hash`: replace with `"sha256:<hex>"` for audit-only equality. `passthrough`: record as-is (NOT recommended for production). Workflow authors MAY override per-workflow via `metadata.complianceConfig.maskingMode`. See observability.md §Privacy classification.

supportedClasses.description: Compliance classes this server applies special handling for. A server MAY accept all five classes but only enforce stricter retention on a subset; this field declares the operational reality so workflow authors can choose a server appropriately.
